title: forestiera retusa
intro: Forestiera retusa, also known as the Mexican elbow, is a deciduous shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Forestiera retusa include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Forestiera retusa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and promote wound healing.
